name: Kukeri Festival
description: Dancers in elaborate costumes and heavy bells scaring away evil spirits and ensuring harvest.

name: Surva Festival
description: Largest Kukeri celebration where groups from across Bulgaria and beyond compete.

name: Kurentovanje
description: Furry mythical figures called Kurents chase away winter and welcome spring.

name: Orthodox Christmas
description: Traditions include burning the badnjak (young oak branch), special foods, and family gatherings.

name: Orthodox New Year
description: Celebration according to the Julian calendar.

name: čobanska nova godina (Shepherd's New Year)
description: Rural celebrations with bonfires, folk music, and feasting.

name: Martenitsa
description: Tradition of exchanging red and white thread ornaments to welcome spring and symbolize health.

name: Lazarice
description: Young girls in traditional costumes sing blessing songs at houses to mark the approach of Easter.

name: Đurđevdan (St. George's Day)
description: Important celebration with ritual bathing, home decoration, and roast lamb feasting, particularly for Roma communities.

name: Nestinarstvo (Fire Dancing)
description: Barefoot dancers carrying icons and walking on glowing embers in a trance-like state.

name: Guča Trumpet Festival
description: Massive brass band competition and celebration with folk music, dancing, and feasting.

name: Galičnik Wedding Festival
description: Traditional Macedonian wedding recreation with authentic costumes and rituals.

name: Grape Harvest Festivals
description: Wine-producing regions celebrate with grape stomping, tastings, and folk performances.

name: Slava
description: Family celebration of a patron saint's day with specific food and rituals.
